verification.target_vocab_notes: そっち appears at the beginning of the sentence as a standalone word

verification.difficulty_notes: Complex conditional structure, idiomatic expression, abstract concept requiring cultural context. Multiple clauses with conditional logic.

verification.target_vocab_notes: あの appears as demonstrative modifying 古い時計

verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ grammar (ても concessive). Too advanced for N5 despite target vocabulary being present

verification.target_vocab_notes: あの appears as demonstrative modifying 仕事の口

verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: は particle, まだ (still), ある existence verb, よ sentence-ending particle

verification.overall_notes: Grammar is N5, but 仕事の口 is somewhat idiomatic and may be challenging. Consider if this idiom is appropriate for N5 learners

verification.target_vocab_notes: たのみ (nominalized form of 頼む) appears in the sentence

verification.overall_notes: Grammar is technically N5, but the nominalized form with polite prefix お may be slightly advanced. The んです construction adds complexity

verification.target_vocab_notes: 頼んだ (ta-form of 頼む) appears in the sentence

verification.difficulty_notes: Short but uses emphatic ぞ particle which may be less common for beginners. Requires cultural understanding of this direct/masculine speech style.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is N5 but ぞ particle may be unfamiliar to beginners. Consider if masculine/casual register is appropriate

verification.target_vocab_notes: 頼んだ (ta-form of 頼む) appears in the sentence

verification.target_vocab_notes: いる appears in て-form continuous している (is starting to rain)

verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: その上 (besides/moreover), continuous ている. The verb structure 降り出す is compound but acceptable
verification.difficulty_notes: Uses compound verb 降り出す (to begin to fall/start raining). Conjunction その上 adds complexity. More advanced vocabulary and structure within N5.
verification.overall_notes: Kana error with 降 reading. Compound verb 降り出す may be borderline for N5. Consider if this compound is appropriate
